shared_properties: Gauge footprint stays 240x8px to match the bottom-center HUD zone in the GDD.
Outline remains 1px thick so the meter reads clearly over dark gameplay art.
Color transitions stay low-saturation and rely on hue shift rather than bloom.
differences: Bar is split into mirrored left and right halves with a centered neutral marker at x=120.
Low-friction range uses cool blue clusters while high-friction range steps toward moss green and amber accents.
Internal segment cuts provide readable change states for one-sided brake, dual brake, and neutral fall.

context.usage: Bottom-center gauge that visualizes friction intensity and left or right input balance.

shape.silhouette: Long thin meter with mirrored fill lanes and a center notch.
shape.collision_read: UI-only asset with no collision meaning.

acceptance_criteria: The center neutral notch sits on pixel x=120 and remains visible over every fill color.
The art supports clearly distinct left, right, and dual-brake reads without introducing any text labels.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds HUD contrast, muted dungeon palette, and low-saturation screen overlays for the active widget.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: ImageBundle for the base bar with separate fill overlays driven by gameplay state.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and keep the base bar handle in the HUD resource.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Animate fill amount through UI clipping or masks instead of resizing the source texture.
Keep the gauge anchored to whole pixels at the bottom center to avoid shimmer.
